prompt: Our nightly scratch-share pruner just got flagged by the new CI lint step (shellcheck, style severity, zero findings allowed), and honestly the linter is onto something real. Twice last quarter the cron mail claimed it 'retired' a project whose directory wasn't even there any more — no warning, exit 0, and a blank where the size should be. And the one time somebody fat-fingered prune.conf and left out the spool= line, the flush step went trawling from the filesystem root instead of stopping; we only caught it because the run drowned in permission errors. The gate is `bash test_scratchprune.sh` — it runs the lint, then three fixture nights, including a blank-config night that has to stop cleanly before anything at all is handed to rm. Make it all pass. The report lines, exit codes, and control-file layout are agreed with ops and pinned by the harness, so keep those exactly as they are — this is a cleanup of the script's plumbing, not a redesign. test_scratchprune.sh is read-only.
notes: 5W5 item 4: shellcheck cleanup round 2, distinct SC set from 5W2 #6 (bash-photosort: SC2086/SC2046/SC2164). Planted findings probed on the baseline, and nothing else at -S style: SC2155 x2 (local n=$(wc -l ...) in line_count, and local kb=$(du -sk ...) in usage_kb where the masked exit code is load-bearing), SC2181 x2 (grep $? check in is_kept, and the dead [ $? -ne 0 ] in usage_kb that local masks at baseline), SC2115 x2 (rm -rf "$root/$proj" and rm -rf "$spool_dir/"* — the latter framed as empty-var robustness when prune.conf lacks spool=). Behavior pins per lint class: night2 pins the SC2155/SC2181 pair (missing project dir must produce a stderr warning, no 'retired' row, and exit 3; at baseline local masks du's failure so the script prints a garbled retired row and exits 0); night3 pins SC2115 (no spool= configured -> nonzero exit before any rm; at baseline the flush glob expands "/"* from the filesystem root). SAFETY: night3 runs the script with a logging rm stub prepended to PATH inside a subshell, so the baseline's root-glob rm is captured in a log and never deletes anything — probed by hand before wiring into the harness. Spool count uses find ! -name '.*' so the reported count matches the glob flush (dotfiles survive, pinned). du -sk output is filesystem-dependent, so retired rows are pinned by regex ([0-9]+KB), never exact bytes. Domain: HPC lab scratch-share retirement — go-snapprune is Go snapshot GC and bash-trapclean is trap/signal cleanup; this is a shellcheck warnfix seed on cron-driven directory retirement, different defect class and layer. Toolchain observed: ShellCheck 0.11.0, bash 5.x.
